page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Eadsv7 family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per physical core. Storage includes a 3780 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. The virtualization layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng falls in the average tier, while PassMark CPU and memory scores are strong, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 3380.11 GB/sec, though large block read and write bandwidths remain average. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier performance for small models but drop to average or poor for larger models due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32ads_v7 offers high memory density and substantial local storage capacity, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of GPU accelerators. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for large-scale AI training or heavy LLM inference. This server is well-suited for memory-optimized databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise applications, and data-intensive processing tasks that benefit from high single-core speeds and large memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Eadsv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. It features 512.0 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression, alongside strong PassMark CPU and memory scores. While memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, large block transfers and multi-core stress tests perform at average levels. Lacking a dedicated GPU, the server is not designed for heavy AI workloads but offers high resource density for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for large databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory and fast local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is an x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, extensive local storage, and top-tier single-core performance for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
512 GB of RAM providing a high 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications
